Premium.name: Demiurge Stella
Premium.review: Sturdy AoE dps with a fixed damage chain finisher. She does everything she needs to at 3-star, so her transcends are mostly a stat bonus. Her burst 3 is very expensive, the barrier at 6-star will rarely show its worth in PvE and may be a negative in arena against certain teams.

Premium.name: Demiurge Astei
Premium.review: Dark dps who revives to get in another S3 before she falls. If she scores a kill she gets to live. This gimmick won't be useful very often in PvE, but it makes her a strong option in PvP. Her burst 3 resets skill cooldowns, including the revive effect, allowing her to use her S3 more often. Every transcend increases her damage output, but doesn't really add more tools to work with.

Premium.name: Demiurge Drakhan
Premium.review: AoE damage reduction, barrier and penetration buffs. She brings all the tools she needs to in PvE out of the box. Her transcends let her become a threat in arena as she can prevent revivals when killing someone with her S3.

Premium.name: Demiurge Vlada
Premium.review: A debuffer mainly bringing a buff removal & unhealable. Her 4- and 5-star reduce enemy priority gains by up to 50%, which is important against world bosses. She needs to AoE using her S3 or burst 3 to be any good as a damage dealer, so her damage potential is locked behind her 5-star (and there are better options for damage). The speed bonus at 6-star is mainly for arena, it reduces gear requirements in PvE, but is rarely needed.

Premium.name: Monad Eva
Premium.review: Healer with dual attacks, the most versatile support in the game. 5-star gives her access to an unconditional dual attack with 2 heroes, a cleanse and revival buffs. 6-star's Seal debuff has its uses in arena and some of the world bosses, but has very little use when starting out in PvE.

Premium.name: Gnosis Beth
Premium.review: Effectiveness based DoT dps. A staple in several guild raids and consistently strong in many turn limited fights. 4-star increases her DoT damage by 50% and increases her survivability a lot, making it her most important transcend level when building her. 6-star applies 2 turns of Eternal Bleeding at the start of battle and makes her Dominion permanent, allowing her to open the fight with high burst damage.

Premium.name: Demiurge Delta
Premium.review: Lots of cleansing and support with priority boosts and free burst skills for the main dps (or all dark heroes except healers at 6-star). Doesn't gain a lot in PvE outside of the 6-star effect, so transcending her isn't a priority here. Her resilience buff and priority boosts can make her a threat in arena, along with her teamwide +10 speed bonus.

Premium.name: Demiurge Luna
Premium.review: Very high damage potential while being very sturdy herself. Competitive at 3-star with each transcend giving her a bigger edge. When left on auto she casts her S2 at every opportunity, so she can waste a lot of turns in turn limited content.

Limited.name: Poolside Trickster Regina
Limited.review: Great water dps with the rare buff steal and resource steal. Her skill priority gains can be applied frequently, so she gets a lot of turns. The increased damage against buffed targets makes her very strong on auto in turn-limited fights. 4-star increases her priority gains and makes her burst cheaper. 5-star gives her a buff steal on S3 that she can use very often, and 6-star adds team-wide priority gains at the start of battle. This isn't needed for her to be one of Water's best damage dealers.

Limited.name: Kitsune of Eternity Tamamo-no-Mae
Limited.review: Curse detonation removes the main threat to Curse comps — enemies cleansing curses. Tamamo’s curses are irremovable, letting her fight a few bosses that clean themselves. However, curse damage can be too slow for turn-limited content. In PvE, her 3★ and 4★ are very impactful for the detonation playstyle. 5★ doesn’t add much there. 6★ adds a taunt and damage reduction which are nice QoL, but not a big asset in arena, where this would be the most notable.

Limited.name: Holy Night's Blessing Dianne
Limited.review: Offensive buff support with good damage. 3★ gives a strong party buff that pairs well with many DPS. 4★ and 5★ give her more uptime and consistency. 6★ puts her in the top row of Fire element's damage dealers.

Limited.name: Ryu Lion
Limited.review: Excellent single-target DPS in both PvE and PvP. He has high base stats and good modifiers; with 4★/5★ she reaches an impressive breakpoint for most content. At 6★ she gets a speed buff at battle start and extra accuracy, improving arena reliability.

Limited.name: Bell Cranel
Limited.review: Strong burn hero with very high attack potential. His burns stack quickly in PvE and can carry turn-limited fights when built properly. 4★/5★ improve consistency and damage. In PvP his survivability is limited; 6★ helps but some teams have extremely high defense, so the difference may not be very visible.

Limited.name: Ais Wallenstein
Limited.review: Free collab hero, was able to reach 6★ during the event. She’s a reliable Light DPS with simple, effective skills. With proper gear she can be competitive with other Light DPS when building a team around her.
